common_patterns.overview: Reusable patterns for diagnostic-first debugging across different scenarios.

pattern.name: diagnostic_instrumentation_autonomous
pattern.description: Adding [DEBUG] instrumentation and collecting output autonomously (hands-off mode)
pattern.context: Evidence is inconclusive and hands_off_mode is true. Cannot ask user for help.

steps.step: Add targeted [DEBUG] statements to the code paths identified as potentially problematic
Re-run the failing tests (or the relevant command) to execute the instrumented code
Collect the output from the test run
Analyze: do the debug logs reveal the root cause?
If still unclear: add more instrumentation to narrower code paths, re-run, repeat

pattern.limit: After 3 rounds of increasingly targeted instrumentation without identifying root cause: document what was instrumented and what remains unknown. Escalate to spawner. Do not loop indefinitely.

cleanup.step: Remove ALL [DEBUG] statements after identifying root cause

pattern.name: diagnostic_instrumentation_interactive
pattern.description: Adding [DEBUG] instrumentation with user-assisted reproduction (interactive mode)
pattern.context: Evidence is inconclusive and hands_off_mode is false. Can ask user for help.

steps.step: Add targeted [DEBUG] statements to the code paths identified as potentially problematic

Tell the user: - Exact steps to reproduce the issue - Where the debug output will appear (use environment-appropriate guidance): - Browser apps: DevTools Console (F12) - Backend servers / CLI tools: the terminal running the process - Mobile apps: Logcat (Android) or Xcode console (iOS) - Desktop apps: debug console or launching terminal - Ask them to copy the output back to you
Wait for user to provide output. Do not prompt again unprompted if the user doesn't respond.
Analyze: do the debug logs reveal the root cause?
If still unclear: add more targeted instrumentation, ask user to reproduce again

pattern.limit: After 3 rounds of increasingly targeted instrumentation without identifying root cause: present findings to user with options: "Investigate from a different angle", "Provide more context", "I'll investigate manually." Do not loop indefinitely.

cleanup.step: Remove ALL [DEBUG] statements after identifying root cause

pattern.name: single_issue_fix_flow
pattern.description: Complete flow for fixing a single issue from analysis to completion

flow.step: If not: instrument → collect output → identify root cause (3-round limit)
Mandatory instrumentation on retry if previous no-instrumentation fix failed (with unavailable-output escape)
Apply minimal fix (E2E tests never modified; unit/integration tests updated if they depended on buggy behavior)
Run unit + integration tests (E2E only at final gate) → fix ALL fixable failures
Verify: tests green (hands-off) or user confirms (interactive)
If verification fails: re-enter at step 1 (instrumentation mandatory if skipped before → go to step 2; if already instrumented → refine → go to step 4)
Run full test suite → distinguish regression-test-bug from fix-bug → all green → done

pattern.name: multi_issue_triage_and_fix
pattern.description: Complete flow for fixing multiple issues from triage to batch completion

flow.step: Triage: analyze all issues → order by dependencies, risk, impact
Present order to user (interactive) or proceed (hands-off)
For each issue: follow single-issue flow (steps 1-6 only, skip regression test)
If any issue hits circuit breaker: preserve verified fixes, write regression tests for them, report partial success
Combined test run: all fixes together → catch cross-fix interactions → fix if needed
Final verification: user confirms all (interactive) or combined suite green (hands-off)
Run full test suite → all fixable tests green → done

pattern.name: test_failure_investigation
pattern.description: Investigating a test failure using the test output as primary evidence
pattern.context: Debug was spawned because a test (unit, integration, or E2E) is failing.

approach.step: Read the failing test code to understand what it asserts
Read the assertion error — what value was expected vs. what was received?
Read the code under test — trace the logic path that produces the wrong value
Use the stack trace to identify the exact line where the failure originates
If the test output clearly shows the discrepancy: fix the source code
If the test output is ambiguous: add [DEBUG] instrumentation to trace the execution path

e2e_special_handling.rule: For E2E test failures: read the e2e-authoring-guide.md if available (from spawner context) to understand test conventions
E2E failures may have longer execution chains — start instrumentation at the boundary between components
Write a unit/integration regression test for the root cause, not an E2E test
Run E2E suite only at the final gate (step 8), not at step 5 — avoid double runs

pattern.name: smoke_test_failure_investigation
pattern.description: Investigating a smoke test failure reported by a user
pattern.context: Debug was spawned because a human-verified smoke test failed. User described what went wrong.

approach.step: Read the user's description of the failure carefully
Read the relevant source code based on the feature context (provided in spawn prompt)
Try to reproduce the issue by analyzing the code paths
If root cause is not obvious from code: add [DEBUG] instrumentation and guide the user to reproduce
After fix: ask user to verify with exact reproduction steps
After user confirms: write regression test

pattern.hands_off_note: Hands-off mode with smoke tests is NOT supported — smoke tests require human verification. If spawned in hands-off mode without an automated test that reproduces the issue: report that autonomous verification is impossible. The spawner should either provide an automated reproduction test or use interactive mode.

pattern.name: circuit_breaker
pattern.description: When to stop trying and escalate
pattern.trigger: Same test(s) keep failing after 3 genuinely different fix approaches
pattern.counter_scope: Each test tracks its own failure counter. Step 5 and step 8 share the same counter for the same test — a failure at step 5 counts toward the same test's 3-attempt limit.

genuinely_different_criteria.description: What distinguishes a genuinely different approach from a variation:

different.example: Fix the data layer vs. fix the business logic vs. fix the presentation layer
Change the algorithm vs. add input validation vs. fix the data source
Fix a race condition with a lock vs. restructure to avoid shared state vs. use atomic operations

not_different.example: Adjusting a timeout from 100ms to 200ms to 300ms
Changing a constant value incrementally until tests pass

response.action: Report: what root cause was suspected, all 3 approaches tried, why each failed, current state
In hands-off mode: report via completion summary (spawner handles escalation)
In interactive mode: present findings to user and ask how to proceed

pattern.name: multi_issue_partial_circuit_breaker
pattern.description: Handling circuit breaker in multi-issue mode without losing verified fixes
pattern.trigger: During multi-issue fix loop (step 2), one issue hits its circuit breaker

response.step: Preserve all previously-verified fixes — do NOT revert them
Write regression tests for all verified fixes immediately
Run the full test suite for the verified fixes to confirm they're green
Report partial success: "[N] issues fixed with regression tests. [1] issue unresolved: [description]. Circuit breaker triggered: [summary]."
Do NOT lose verified work. Do NOT continue trying the unresolved issue.
